sources.page: Entry 5030 (as Lucy M. Hooker spouse, listed as "David L. Hebner" — middle initial discrepancy)

death.cause: Killed in action — hit by a shell while serving with the 309th Infantry Regiment, 78th Division, U.S. Army. The 78th Division had moved into the Saint-Mihiel salient on September 16-17, 1918, relieving the 89th Division after the main Saint-Mihiel offensive (Sept 12-15) had captured the salient. Jay was killed during the consolidation/relief phase, two days after the offensive officially ended and nine days before the Meuse-Argonne offensive opened. Age 23.

burial.plot_notes: Saint-Mihiel American Cemetery and Memorial, Thiaucourt-Regniéville, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lorraine, France — Plot B Row 24 Grave 31. One of 4,153 American soldiers buried there. CENOTAPH at Boxwood Cemetery, Medina NY (CEM150) installed 2016 by his great-niece Debbie Morse. Note: One Findagrave listing erroneously shows Boxwood Medina as primary burial — but the cenotaph notation clarifies he is buried in France with a memorial stone in Medina.

sources.short: Findagrave (Jay David Hebner memorial — Saint-Mihiel American Cemetery, France)
Findagrave secondary listing (Boxwood Cemetery cenotaph, Medina NY)
Tom Rivers (Editor), 'Gravestone coming for Medina man killed in World War I,' Orleans Hub, May 28, 2016

narrative_blocks.header: KIA in France two days after Saint-Mihiel
narrative_blocks.body: Jay David Hebner was born June 2, 1895 in Le Roy NY (the Genesee County town his mother Lucy's family had ties to via his great-grandfather William Shader). He grew up in Medina at 920 Gwinn Street. On December 4, 1917, age 22, he enlisted in Company F, 74th Infantry of the New York Guard, training at the Armory in Medina.

Jay was buried at Saint-Mihiel American Cemetery and Memorial in Thiaucourt-Regniéville, Lorraine, France — Plot B, Row 24, Grave 31, one of 4,153 American soldiers there. His parents likely could not afford to bring him home or to buy a stone in Medina. For nearly a century there was no memorial in his hometown.

cross_connections.display_label: the president's son, an aviator downed over France in 1918

burial.plot_notes: Boxwood Cemetery, Medina Orleans County NY — same area as husband Irving and parents David + Lucy Hebner

sources.page: Buried Boxwood Cemetery Medina NY. Findagrave records mother as "LOUISE HOOKER" — informal alternate to "Lucy."

burial.plot_notes: Boxwood Cemetery, Medina Orleans County NY

narrative_blocks.header: WWII Armored Division soldier in Europe
narrative_blocks.body: Clarence Irving 'Cookie' Cook was born February 1, 1918 in Medina NY — barely a month before the U.S. War Department was authorizing the operations that would lead to his Uncle Jay Hebner's death in France that September. Twenty-five years later, Clarence would follow his uncle into European combat. He served in the U.S. Army's Armored Division in the European Theater during World War II.

burial.plot_notes: Boxwood Cemetery, Medina Orleans County NY

sources.short: Findagrave (Donald David Cook memorial, Boxwood Cemetery Medina)

Tom Rivers (Editor), 'Gravestone coming for Medina man killed in World War I,' Orleans Hub, May 28, 2016 — references Don Cook as Debbie Morse's father, prominent local photographer, WWII veteran, d.2004

narrative_blocks.header: Prominent Medina photographer and WWII veteran
narrative_blocks.body: Donald David 'Cookie' Cook was born January 23, 1922 in Medina NY, four years after his uncle Jay Hebner was killed in France. Don served in World War II and returned to become a prominent local photographer in Orleans County — the visual chronicler of mid-20th-century Medina life. He died November 20, 2004 in his hometown at age 82, buried at Boxwood Cemetery near his parents and his great-grandparents the Hebners.

narrative_blocks.header: His daughter Debbie restored Jay's memory
narrative_blocks.body: Don's daughter Dabrina D. 'Debbie' Cook Morse — Don's youngest of four — was the person who in 2016 finally brought a memorial to Medina for her great-uncle Jay Hebner. Don had told Debbie about the uncle killed in WWI; after Don's 2004 death, Debbie and her husband Jim researched Jay's records, located his Saint-Mihiel American Cemetery grave in France, and paid out of pocket for a cenotaph at Boxwood Cemetery near the family Hebner and Cook plots. As Debbie said in May 2016: 'It's about bringing honor to him.

narrative_blocks.header: Married third cousin from Myron Hooker line
narrative_blocks.body: Thomas Harvey Hooker married Caroline E. 'Carrie' Shader on February 12, 1863. Both spouses were third cousins descending from Captain Thomas Hooker — Thomas Harvey through his father Thomas Edwin Hooker, and Caroline through her mother Laura Electa Hooker Shader whose father Myron Hooker was Thomas Edwin's half-brother. The cousin-marriage united two Hooker descent paths that had separated several generations earlier.

narrative_blocks.header: His cousin marriage merged two separated Hooker lines
narrative_blocks.body: Thomas Harvey Hooker's marriage to his third cousin Caroline Shader re-merged two Hooker family lines that had separated three generations earlier through the half-brother split between his father Thomas Edwin and her grandfather Myron. The cousin marriage was a deliberate or coincidental rejoin of two parallel Hooker descent streams in western New York. He and his wife maintained the Hooker surname throughout their marriage.

narrative_blocks.header: Eldest son of Thomas Edwin Hooker
narrative_blocks.body: Thomas Harvey Hooker was born July 15, 1840 in Murray, Orleans County New York — eldest son of Thomas Edwin Hooker and Lucy Hyde Hooker. The Hooker family had migrated to the western New York lake-plain region during the early nineteenth century along with many other Connecticut Yankees seeking new agricultural land.

narrative_blocks.header: Born Orleans County NY, died Stafford NY 1919
narrative_blocks.body: Thomas Harvey Hooker died March 21, 1919 in Stafford, Genesee County New York at age seventy-eight. His two children — Lucy and Alvah — both lived into the mid-twentieth century, with Alvah surviving until 1953 and Lucy until 1924.

burial.plot_notes: Stafford Rural Cemetery, Stafford Genesee County NY — same plot area as wife Caroline + daughter Alva + son-in-law Jean Mason + infant grandson H. Mason

birth.historical_notes: Per EH 3872: b. March 16, 1842 Wethersfield. WikiTree records "Wethersfield Springs, Wyoming County NY" — same location.

parents.mother_research_notes: Laura E. Hooker Shader (HD3260, b.1818 VT, Myron Hooker's daughter — making Caroline a Hooker descendant on her mother's side)

burial.plot_notes: Stafford Rural Cemetery, Stafford Genesee County NY — alongside husband Thomas Harvey and daughter Alva

cross_connections.evidence: Thomas Edwin's father Thomas Gould (H01270) and Laura's father Myron (H01272) were half-brothers via Capt. Thomas Hooker (H00491) by different mothers. Two of Thomas Edwin's sons each married a Shader-line descendant of Laura: Thomas Harvey (H03872) m. Laura's daughter Caroline E. Shader (X02269); William Henry (H03880) m. Laura's granddaughter Lydia M. Robinson. Two cousin marriages closed the loop between the Thomas Gould and Myron lines.
cross_connections.display_label: Their children's marriages closed two Hooker-cousin loops
Her daughter Caroline Shader married his son Thomas Harvey Hooker
cross_connections.notes: Cousin marriage documentation. H02424 (Thomas Edwin Hooker) and HD3260 (Laura Electa Hooker Shader) are uncle and niece — Laura's father Myron Hooker (H01272) was Thomas Edwin's half-brother. Their respective children H03872 Thomas Harvey Hooker (Thomas Edwin's son) and X02269 Caroline E. Shader (Laura's daughter) were third cousins who married Feb 12, 1863. Children of that marriage (H05030 Lucy and H05031 Alvah) inherit double-Hooker ancestry with descent_paths_to_thomas=2.

burial.plot_notes: Sandy Creek Cemetery Murray Orleans County New York per FA
Sandy Creek Cemetery, Murray, Orleans County NY (per Findagrave). Same as husband Thomas Edwin.

birth.historical_notes: Per Findagrave: b.1808 Rhinebeck Dutchess County NY. WikiTree had recorded father as Peter Shader of Wethersfield Springs Wyoming Co NY — different location. Possibly William moved there as a young man before marrying Laura (1836).

parents.father_research_notes: Peter Shader (b.1782 Wethersfield Springs Wyoming Co NY) per WikiTree

burial.plot_notes: Beach Ridge Cemetery, Brockport Monroe County NY — Section 2 (unmarked)

bio.extra_names: Not in EH 1272 — almost certainly an EH omission. Per WikiTree (which lists her as Betsy Bacon's daughter, likely confused with Sally Brooks who was the biological mother).
bio.bio_blurb: Vermont-born Hooker daughter who lived to ninety-three

birth.historical_notes: Per WikiTree: b.1818 Vermont — making her older than her EH-listed siblings Myron Smith (1820) and Thomas Cullen (1822). Likely Myron Hooker + Sally Brooks first child; EH 1272 omitted her, possibly because she had no Hooker-surname descendants to track.

parents.mother_research_notes: Per Findagrave: Betsey Bacon Hooker (X02278). EARLIER hypothesis was Sally Brooks (X02277) based on timing logic (Laura b.1818, Sally Brooks's children Myron Smith b.1820 and Thomas Cullen b.1822). Findagrave likely correct that Betsey is the BIOLOGICAL mother — meaning Betsey may have been Myron's first wife, OR Laura was Betsey's daughter from her earlier Bacon marriage who took Hooker surname after her mother remarried Myron. EH 1272 omitted Laura entirely. UNRESOLVED — going with Findagrave per primary memorial source.

narrative_blocks.header: She buried the genealogist’s mistake by outliving everyone
narrative_blocks.body: Born in frontier Vermont in 1818, she lived to ninety-three — long enough to see the railroad, the telegraph, the Civil War, and electric light reach the country towns of her girlhood. One of her daughters, Violetta, raised thirteen children of her own.

cross_connections.evidence: Laura's father Myron Hooker and Thomas Edwin's father Thomas Gould were half-brothers via Capt. Thomas Hooker (H00491). Two of Thomas Edwin's sons (Thomas Harvey + William Henry) each married into Laura's line — closing two cousin loops.
cross_connections.display_label: Two of his sons married into her descendants
His son Thomas Harvey Hooker married her daughter Caroline Shader
cross_connections.notes: Cousin marriage documentation. HD3260 (Laura Electa Hooker Shader) and H02424 (Thomas Edwin Hooker) are niece and uncle — Laura's father Myron Hooker (H01272) was Thomas Edwin's half-brother. Their respective children X02269 Caroline E. Shader (Laura's daughter) and H03872 Thomas Harvey Hooker (Thomas Edwin's son) were third cousins who married Feb 12, 1863.

burial.plot_notes: Beach Ridge Cemetery, Brockport Monroe County NY — Section 2 Lot 56 (unmarked grave)

burialCemetery.name: Boxwood Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Long-standing Medina NY cemetery serving Orleans County. The Hebner-Cook family plot complex includes David Conrad Hebner (1863-1930), his wife Lucy M. Hooker Hebner (1864-1924), their daughter Etta E. Hebner Cook (1896-1957), her husband Irving Burns Cook (1895-1968), and their two sons Clarence Irving 'Cookie' Cook (1918-1977) and Donald David 'Cookie' Cook (1922-2004), plus Donald's wife Shirley Marie Perry Cook (1936-1967). In 2016, a CENOTAPH for PVT Jay David Hebner (1895-1918, KIA WWI in France, actually buried at Saint-Mihiel American Cemetery) was added to the Hebner-Cook plot complex through the efforts of Jay's great-niece Debbie Morse (Dabrina D. Cook Morse) — Donald's daughter — restoring his memory to Medina after nearly 100 years of absence from the village.
